experiments.title: Exact scaling exponent table
experiments.hypothesis: The recorded velocity, vorticity, pressure, and force exponents follow from one exact scaling rule.
experiments.certificate: Unit-test assertions are the algebraic certificate.
experiments.success_criterion: All canonical critical and energy exponents equal their exact rational targets.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The dossier or implementation has a scaling error.

experiments.title: 2607 logarithmic exponent bookkeeping
experiments.hypothesis: The paper's stated distribution and rearrangement exponents transform algebraically as claimed.
invariants: It does not prove O'Neil or energy inequalities
experiments.success_criterion: Vorticity tail (3/2,3/2) maps to rearrangement (2/3,1) and velocity tail (3,3).
experiments.failure_interpretation: At least one claimed exponent conversion is algebraically inconsistent.

experiments.title: Pinned arXiv 2607.08866v2 source cache
experiments.hypothesis: The exact TeX source can be reproducibly fetched, hashed, and safely extracted.
invariants: No tracked copy without redistribution permission
experiments.method: navier_lab.source_cache fetches the arXiv e-print and writes a manifest.
experiments.certificate: SHA-256 manifest plus archive member list.
experiments.success_criterion: The manifest names chaos_sphere.tex and records a nonempty SHA-256 digest.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The source version is not pinned or the archive layout changed.

experiments.title: Source-anchored audit of 2607 foundation obligations
experiments.hypothesis: The exact v2 source permits a bounded verdict on O2607-01 through O2607-04 without assuming any downstream commutator estimate.
invariants: Every source display and imported theorem is line-anchored
The main conditional theorem remains unverified
experiments.method: Parse the complete TeX source, rewrite all foundation quantifiers, track constant dependencies, and independently derive the tensor cancellation.
experiments.certificate: The proof map and its linked analytic counterexamples and tensor derivation.
experiments.success_criterion: Each of O2607-01 through O2607-04 receives a verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict with exact scope.
experiments.failure_interpretation: At least one foundation quantifier, dependency, or tensor identity remains unresolved.

experiments.title: Weak-L(3/2) multi-core localization counterexample
experiments.hypothesis: A uniform weak-L(3/2) bound alone does not confine every high-level set to one ball of radius proportional to lambda^(-1/2).
invariants: Divergence-free realization is not needed for the first measure-theoretic counterexample
A later PDE-consistent version is separate
experiments.method: Construct separated critical spikes with controlled distribution function and unbounded covering diameter.
experiments.certificate: A written proof and executable distribution/covering check.
experiments.success_criterion: The example has bounded weak-L(3/2) quasi-norm while violating single-ball containment uniformly.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The proposed spike weights or geometry do not preserve the critical bound.

experiments.title: Weak-L(3/2) one-core anisotropic localization counterexample
experiments.hypothesis: A uniform weak-L(3/2) bound does not force even one connected point-centered high-level set into a ball of radius proportional to lambda^(-1/2).
invariants: The example is scalar and makes no PDE-consistency claim
experiments.method: Use anisotropic boxes with half-width powers 1/4, 5/8, and 5/8.
experiments.certificate: A written proof and exact rational/integer exponent checks.
experiments.success_criterion: The scaled distribution tail is constant while the necessary critical-radius covering constant grows as lambda^(1/4).
experiments.failure_interpretation: The anisotropic volume or diameter exponents were calculated incorrectly.

experiments.title: Zero-set obstruction to logarithmic-BMO vorticity direction
experiments.hypothesis: Smooth divergence-free vorticity need not admit a normalized direction with the bmo_(1/|log r|) modulus across its zero set.
invariants: The vorticity is smooth, compactly supported, and divergence-free
Changing values on the measure-zero zero axis cannot change mean oscillation
experiments.method: Take the curl of a compactly supported vector potential that yields (x1,-x2,0) near the origin and compute its scale-invariant directional oscillation.
experiments.certificate: The symmetry calculation gives mean zero and mean oscillation exactly one on every centered small ball.
experiments.success_criterion: The weighted-BMO seminorm diverges for every assignment on the zero axis.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The local direction average or divergence-free construction is incorrect.

experiments.title: Exact Biot-Savart unidirectional strain contraction
experiments.hypothesis: The unidirectional Biot-Savart contribution has zero Rayleigh contraction along the fixed vorticity direction, although its strain tensor need not vanish.
invariants: The human index derivation is the proof and the finite grid is only a check
experiments.method: Differentiate Biot-Savart in indices, symmetrize, contract, and check the finite-dimensional tensor identity with exact integers.
experiments.certificate: A written principal-value derivation, a nonzero-tensor counterexample, and exact unit tests.
experiments.success_criterion: The Rayleigh numerator equals the scalar triple-product form and vanishes for every unidirectional source tested.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The tensor signs, index contractions, or stated cancellation are wrong.

experiments.title: Independent HWY profile and spectrum reproduction
experiments.hypothesis: The published residual and unstable-eigenvalue certificates can be regenerated independently.
experiments.method: Rebuild approximate profile, finite-rank operator, quadrature bounds, and interval spectrum.
experiments.certificate: Machine-readable interval certificate checked by a small independent verifier.
experiments.success_criterion: All analytic bootstrap inequalities close using regenerated certified constants.
experiments.failure_interpretation: Classify the mismatch as implementation, source ambiguity, or theorem-critical gap.
